# VanTripJapan — Campervan Rental & Road Trip Guide for Kyushu, Japan

> VanTripJapan is a family-run campervan rental service based in Fukuoka, Japan. We provide fully-equipped campervans for international travelers to explore Kyushu — Japan's southernmost main island known for hot springs, volcanoes, and stunning coastal roads. All-inclusive pricing starts from ¥16,500/day.

## Why Choose VanTripJapan

- **Family-operated since 2022** — not a faceless agency, but a real team that hand-builds and maintains every van
- **All-inclusive pricing** — insurance (CDW), ETC highway card, camping gear, bedding, English navigation, and 24/7 roadside support included
- **10 minutes from Fukuoka Airport** — the easiest pickup location in Kyushu
- **Multilingual support** — English, French, German, Chinese, Hebrew, and 100+ languages
- **Two vehicle options**: Roof Tent Probox (¥16,500~/day) for couples, and Bed Bongo Brawny (¥22,000~/day) for families
- **No hidden fees** — what you see is what you pay

## Who We Serve

International travelers visiting Kyushu, Japan — especially from Europe (France, Germany, Switzerland, UK, Israel), Australia, Taiwan, and the Americas. Our typical customers are couples and small families who want the freedom to explore Japan at their own pace, away from crowded tourist trains.

## Campervan Rental Details

- **Location**: Hakozaki, Higashi-ku, Fukuoka City (10 min from Fukuoka Airport, direct highway access)
- **Hours**: 9:00–17:00 daily (flexible by arrangement)
- **Pricing**: All-inclusive from ¥16,500/day (Probox) / ¥22,000/day (Bongo)
- **What's included**: Insurance (CDW), ETC card, English GPS navigation, camping chairs, table, cookware, bedding, towels, 24/7 roadside assistance
- **Booking**: Via WhatsApp (+81-70-9375-7129) or email
- **License requirements**: International Driving Permit (IDP) from Geneva Convention countries; JAF translation for Taiwan, Germany, France, Switzerland, Belgium, Monaco

## About Kyushu

Kyushu is Japan's third-largest island and one of its most beautiful. It is home to:
- **Beppu** — Japan's hot spring capital with 2,800+ onsen sources
- **Mount Aso** — the largest active volcano caldera in the world
- **Takachiho Gorge** — a mythical gorge with 17m waterfalls
- **Yakushima** — UNESCO World Heritage ancient cedar forests
- **Nagasaki** — historic port city with unique East-West fusion culture
- **Fukuoka** — Japan's most livable city, famous for Hakata ramen and yatai street food stalls

Kyushu is the ideal region for campervan travel because of its mild climate, compact distances, abundant hot springs, and over 600 campgrounds.

## Associated Services

- **JDLTC** (Japan Driver's License Translation Center): https://drive-japan-license.com/ — Official license translation service for drivers from Switzerland, Germany, France, Belgium, Monaco, and Taiwan
- **キャンプ女子株式会社** (Camp Jyo Inc.): https://www.camjyo.com/ — Our parent company, specializing in outdoor experiences and campground consulting
